html:
[1415]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 建如生云 - Jian speaks as if he were still alive.
  • 願母割哀還去 - Wishes his mother to cut her grief and return.
  • 建今還在寺住 - Jian is still staying at the temple.
  • 母即止哭而還 - The mother stops crying and returns.
  • 舉家菜食長齋 - The whole family eats vegetarian food and observes a long fast.
  • 至閏月十一日 - Refers to a specific date in the lunar calendar.
  • 賢明夢見建 - Xianming dreams of Jian.
  • 願父暫出東齋 - Wishes his father to temporarily come out of the eastern study.
  • 賢明便香湯自浴 - Xianming then bathes himself with fragrant water.
  • 齋戒出東齋 - Observes a fast and comes out of the eastern study.
  • 至十四夜於明中聞建喚聲 - On the night of the fourteenth, he hears Jian's call in the light.
  • 驚起見建在齋前如生時 - He is startled awake and sees Jian in front of the study as he was when alive.
  • 父問汝住在何處 - The father asks where you live.
  • 建云從亡來住在練神宮中 - Jian says he has been living in the Palace of Refining Spirit since his death.
  • 滿百日當得生忉利天 - After a hundred days, he will be reborn in the Heaven of the Thirty-Three.
  • 建不忍見父母兄弟哭泣傷恸 - Jian cannot bear to see his parents and siblings cry and grieve.
  • 三七日禮諸佛菩薩請四天王 - For twenty-one days, he worships the Buddhas and Bodhisattvas and invites the Four Heavenly Kings.
  • 願父母從今已後勿復啼哭 - Wishes his parents not to cry anymore from now on.
  • 祭祠阿母已發願求見建 - Has already made a vow to worship and seek to see Jian.
  • 不久當命終即共建同生一处 - Soon he will die and Jian will be reborn together with him.
  • 父壽可得七十三命終後當三年受罪報 - The father's lifespan is seventy-three years, after which he will suffer three years of punishment.
  • 勤苦行道可得免脫 - By diligently practicing the path, he can be freed.
  • 問曰汝從夜中來那得有光明 - Asked how he could have light coming from the night.
  • 建曰今與菩薩諸天同下此其身光耳 - Jian says that he is coming down with the Bodhisattvas and heavenly beings, and this is his own light.
  • 又問曰汝天上識誰 - Asked again who Jian knows in heaven.
  • 建曰見王車騎張吳興外祖宋西河 - Jian says he saw Wang Cheqi, Zhang Wuxing, and his maternal grandfather, Song Xihé.
  • 建曰非但此一门中生从四十七年 - Jian says not only from this family, but from forty-seven years.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 閏月十一日 - A specific date in the lunar calendar, indicating the importance of the timing in the narrative.
  • 練神宮 - Refers to a celestial palace where spirits are refined and purified.
  • 忉利天 - Refers to the Heaven of the Thirty-Three, a level in Buddhist cosmology.
  • 三七日 - Refers to twenty-one days, a significant period in religious and spiritual practices.
  • 四天王 - Refers to the Four Heavenly Kings, protectors of the four cardinal directions in Buddhist cosmology.
  • 王車騎 - A title or name of a high-ranking official or noble.
  • 張吳興 - A name, possibly referring to a historical figure from Wu Prefecture.
  • 宋西河 - A name, possibly referring to a historical figure from the Western River region.
  • 四十七年 - A reference to a significant period, possibly indicating a span of time or a generational span.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • This phrase is elliptical and must be read as Jian speaking as if he were still alive, wishing his mother to cut her grief and return.
  • This sentence is a compound statement, indicating Jian's current residence and the mother's subsequent actions.
  • This phrase is a temporal marker followed by a dream narrative.
  • This sequence of actions must be read as a series of steps taken by Xianming.
  • This compound sentence describes a sequence of events in the night.
  • This dialogue must be read as a question and answer exchange.
  • This compound sentence describes a future event and Jian's emotional state.
  • This phrase indicates a specific period of religious practice.
  • This compound sentence expresses wishes and actions.
  • This compound sentence describes a future event and the father's lifespan and punishment.
  • This phrase indicates a condition for liberation.
  • This is a question asking about the source of light.
  • This is a response explaining the source of light.
  • This is another question asking about Jian's acquaintances in heaven.
  • This is a response listing the people Jian knows.
  • This is a response indicating a broader scope of time or generation.

Proper Nouns

  • 建 - A proper name, likely referring to a person.
  • 賢明 - A proper name, likely referring to a person.
  • 王車騎 - A title or name, likely referring to a high-ranking official or noble.
  • 張吳興 - A name, likely referring to a historical figure from Wu Prefecture.
  • 宋西河 - A name, likely referring to a historical figure from the Western River region.
  • 練神宮 - A proper noun, referring to a celestial palace.
  • 忉利天 - A proper noun, referring to the Heaven of the Thirty-Three.
  • 四天王 - A proper noun, referring to the Four Heavenly Kings.
  • 閏月十一日 - A specific date in the lunar calendar.
  • 三七日 - A specific period of twenty-one days.
  • 四十七年 - A specific period of forty-seven years.

[1416]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 七死七生 - Refers to a cycle of seven deaths and rebirths, often used in Buddhist or Taoist contexts to describe a process of spiritual purification or transformation.
  • 四道果 - Refers to the four stages of enlightenment in Buddhism: Stream-Enterer (Sotapanna), Once-Returner (Sakadagami), Non-Returner (Anagami), and Arahant.
  • 先發七願 - Means 'previously made seven vows,' indicating a commitment or promise made earlier.
  • 臨盡時 - Means 'at the time of death' or 'when nearing the end of life.'
  • 七處生死 - Refers to the seven realms of existence or states of being, often associated with the seven heavens in Buddhist cosmology.
  • 大哭 - Means 'to weep greatly,' expressing intense emotion.
  • 與七家分別也 - Means 'to part ways with the seven families,' indicating a separation or departure.
  • 垂死 - Means 'on the verge of death,' emphasizing the urgency of the situation.
  • 無相值期 - Means 'no meeting or encounter,' suggesting a lack of reunion or connection.
  • 忽然 - Means 'suddenly' or 'abruptly,' indicating a sudden change or disappearance.
  • 忽然不見 - Means 'disappeared suddenly,' describing an abrupt end to visibility.
  • 去後 - Means 'after leaving,' indicating a period following the departure.
  • 猶有 - Means 'still has' or 'remains,' suggesting a lingering effect.
  • 亦並聞 - Means 'also heard,' indicating that others were aware of the same thing.
  • 出家 - Means 'to become a monk or nun,' indicating a religious or spiritual commitment.
  • 名法藏 - Means 'named Fa Zang,' indicating the name of a person or entity.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 七死七生 - This phrase is often used in Buddhist contexts to describe the process of spiritual purification or transformation through multiple rebirths.
  • 四道果 - The four stages of enlightenment in Buddhism, which are significant milestones in the path to Nirvana.
  • 七願 - The seven vows are a common theme in Buddhist and Taoist practices, often related to spiritual goals or commitments.
  • 七處生死 - The seven realms of existence or states of being, often associated with the seven heavens in Buddhist cosmology, represent different levels of spiritual attainment or existence.
  • 大哭 - In Chinese culture, weeping is a common expression of deep emotion, especially in the context of separation or loss.
  • 垂死 - The concept of being on the verge of death is significant in Chinese literature and philosophy, often associated with reflections on life and the afterlife.
  • 無相值期 - The idea of no meeting or encounter is significant in Buddhist philosophy, where it can refer to the impermanence and unpredictability of life.
  • 忽然 - The suddenness of events is a common theme in Chinese literature, often used to describe unexpected changes or endings.
  • 忽然不見 - The abrupt disappearance of someone or something is a common motif in Chinese literature, often used to create a sense of mystery or finality.
  • 去後 - The period following someone's departure is significant in Chinese literature, often used to describe the lingering effects of their presence or absence.
  • 猶有 - The idea of something still remaining or lingering is significant in Chinese literature, often used to describe the lasting impact of a person or event.
  • 亦並聞 - The idea of others also hearing or being aware of something is significant in Chinese literature, often used to create a sense of shared experience or awareness.
  • 出家 - The act of becoming a monk or nun is a significant religious and spiritual commitment in Chinese culture, often associated with renunciation and spiritual pursuit.
  • 名法藏 - The name Fa Zang is significant in Chinese Buddhism, often associated with the *Avatamsaka Sutra* and the *Flower Garland School* of Buddhism.

Proper Nouns

  • 建 - The name of a person, likely a monk or spiritual figure.
  • 江槩 - The name of a family or person.
  • 羊希 - The name of a family or person.
  • 张永 - The name of a family or person.
  • 王 - The name of a family or person.
  • 宋谟 - The name of a family or person.
  • 萧惠明 - The name of a family or person.
  • 梁季文 - The name of a family or person.
  • 贤明 - The name of a person, likely a monk or spiritual figure.
  • 法藏 - The name of a person, likely a monk or spiritual figure, often associated with the *Avatamsaka Sutra* and the *Flower Garland School* of Buddhism.

[1417]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 垂死 - on the verge of death or dying
  • 神汤 - divine soup or sacred broth – likely a symbolic or miraculous substance
  • 平复 - recover or heal
  • 造金像 - make a golden image or create a golden statue
  • 祈命 - pray for life or seek protection for one’s life
  • 刀折而项不伤 - the blade broke but the neck was not harmed – a miraculous event
  • 守道直心 - upholds the Dharma with upright mind – a Buddhist term
  • 汗流地 - sweat flowed to the ground – a miraculous or symbolic phenomenon
  • 异僧 - strange monk or mysterious monk
  • 瓦木土墼 - tiles, wood, and mud bricks – materials used to build a barrier
  • 梦信心者 - the dream of a believer or the dream of one who has faith
  • 木伤其二指 - the wood injured two fingers – a symbolic or literal event
  • 盗像幡葢者 - thieves who stole the image’s canopy and banners
  • 丈八人 - a person of eight zhang (a unit of length) – likely a symbolic or supernatural figure

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 齐竟陵王 - Prince Jingling of Qi – refers to a noble of the Southern Qi Dynasty (479–502 CE), likely a Buddhist patron.
  • 辩正论 - Treatise on Correct Doctrine – a Buddhist text, possibly by Xuanzang or another scholar.
  • 感应传 - Biographies of Karmic Responses – a Buddhist hagiographical text.
  • 法苑珠林 - Jewels of the Dharma Grove – a large Buddhist encyclopedic text compiled by Daoxuan (623 CE).
  • 释僧防 - Monk Shenfang – a Buddhist monk from the Northern Qi dynasty.
  • 高齐 - Northern Qi – one of the Northern dynasties in Chinese history (550–577 CE).
  • 晋州陷日 - the day when Jinzhou fell – likely a historical event involving warfare or conquest.
  • 开皇十年 - the tenth year of Kaihuang – the reign of Emperor Wen of Sui (581–604 CE), so 590 CE.

Proper Nouns

  • 齐竟陵王 - Prince Jingling of Qi – use consistent title and name.
  • 张逸 - Zhang Yi – transliterate as is.
  • 释僧防 - Monk Shenfang – '释' is a prefix for Buddhist monks, so 'Monk Shenfang' is appropriate.
  • 法苑珠林 - Jewels of the Dharma Grove – use the standard English translation.
  • 辩正论 - Treatise on Correct Doctrine – use the standard English title.
  • 感应传 - Biographies of Karmic Responses – use the standard English title.
  • 开皇十年 - the tenth year of Kaihuang – use the standard English rendering of the Sui dynasty era name.

[1418]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 年甫二十 - 甫 means 'just' or 'barely,' so this phrase translates to 'barely twenty years old.'
  • 誓愿 - Refers to a solemn vow or promise.
  • 鸠集金炭 - '鸠集' means 'to gather' or 'to collect,' and '金炭' refers to gold and charcoal, likely used as a metaphor for resources or materials.
  • 周二十年 - '周' means 'to complete' or 'to last,' so this phrase translates to 'lasted for twenty years.'
  • 物力乃办 - '物力' refers to resources or means, and '乃办' means 'were then completed' or 'were then ready.'
  • 大集贤愚 - '大集' means 'great gathering,' and '贤愚' refers to both wise and foolish people.
  • 选日而写像 - '选日' means 'to choose a day,' and '写像' means 'to cast the image' or 'to mold the image.'
  • 烟焰灭息 - '烟焰' refers to smoke and flames, and '灭息' means 'to extinguish' or 'to subside.'
  • 启鑪之后 - '启鑪' means 'to open the furnace,' so this phrase translates to 'after opening the furnace.'
  • 无成 - Means 'no success' or 'no result.'
  • 深自咎责 - '深自咎' means 'to deeply blame oneself,' and '责' means 'to reproach' or 'to accuse.'
  • 稽首懴悔 - '稽首' is a gesture of deep respect or prostration, and '懴悔' means 'to repent' or 'to atone.'
  • 再谋铸造 - '再谋' means 'to plan again,' and '铸造' means 'to cast' or 'to mold.'
  • 精勤艰苦 - '精勤' means 'diligent' or 'assiduous,' and '艰苦' means 'hardship' or 'difficulty.'
  • 事费复备 - '事费' refers to expenses or costs, and '复备' means 'were then ready again.'
  • 启铸其像 - '启铸' means 'to begin casting,' and '其像' means 'the image.'
  • 俟其启鑪 - '俟' means 'to wait,' and '启鑪' means 'to open the furnace.'
  • 金液 - Refers to molten metal or liquid metal used in casting.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 隋开皇中 - Refers to the Kaihuang era of the Sui Dynasty (581–604 CE), a period of significant cultural and political development.
  • 僧澄空 - The name of a Buddhist monk, likely a historical figure, though specific details about him are not provided in the text.
  • 晋阳汾西 - '晋阳' refers to Jinyang, an ancient city in Shanxi Province, and '汾西' means 'west of the Fen River,' indicating a specific location in Shanxi.
  • 铁像 - Refers to a large iron statue, which was a significant undertaking in terms of resources and effort.
  • 大佛 - Refers to a large Buddha statue, often associated with Buddhist temples and religious sites.

Proper Nouns

  • 僧澄空 - The name of the monk, which should be kept consistent throughout the translation.
  • 隋开皇中 - The Kaihuang era of the Sui Dynasty, which should be rendered as 'during the Kaihuang era of the Sui Dynasty.'
  • 晋阳汾西 - The location where the statue was to be cast, which should be rendered as 'in Jinyang, west of the Fen River.'

[1419]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 而舍命焉一以谢于诸佛一以表诚于众善 - Sacrificing one's life to express gratitude to the Buddhas and to demonstrate sincerity to all good deeds. The structure is elliptical, indicating dual purposes.
  • 傥大像圆满后五十年吾当为建重阁耳 - If the large Buddha statue is completed in fifty years, I will build a grand pavilion. The conditional clause sets up the main clause, but the connection is somewhat elliptical.
  • 俄而金液注射赫耀踊跃 - Soon after, molten metal was poured, shining brightly and leaping. The description is vivid and rapid.
  • 计僧死像成日至暠正五十年矣 - Calculating from the day the monk died until Rui Hao's reign was fifty years old. The temporal reference is complex.
  • 以佛法推之则暠也得非澄空之后身欤 - According to Buddhist teachings, then Rui Hao might not be the reincarnation of Qingkong? The final question is philosophical and religious.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 诸佛 - Refers to the Buddhas, central figures in Buddhism.
  • 众善 - Refers to all good deeds, emphasizing the moral and spiritual aspects.
  • 大像圆满 - Refers to the completion of a large Buddha statue, a significant event in Buddhist tradition.
  • 重阁 - A grand pavilion or tower, often built to house or protect important religious artifacts.
  • 唐开元初 - Refers to the early Tang Dynasty, specifically the Kaiyuan period, a time of great cultural and artistic flourishing.
  • 李暠为太原军节度使 - Rui Hao was a military governor (Jiedushi) of Taiyuan during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 平等阁 - A specific name for the pavilion built to house the Buddha statue, emphasizing equality and harmony.

Proper Nouns

  • 澄空 - The name of the monk who built the Buddha statue.
  • 大像 - The large Buddha statue.
  • 李暠 - A historical figure, a military governor during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 太原 - The name of a region in China, now part of modern-day Shanxi Province.
  • 平等阁 - The name of the pavilion built to house the Buddha statue.
  • 唐开元初 - The early Kaiyuan period of the Tang Dynasty.

[1420]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 靈通幽顯 - Means 'spiritually enlightened and profound,' describing a person's spiritual depth.
  • 翹敬尊像 - Means 'reverently worshiping the image,' referring to the practice of showing deep respect to religious images.
  • 事同真佛 - Means 'treating it as if it were a true Buddha,' emphasizing the high regard for the images.
  • 大有悟解 - Means 'he had great insights,' indicating his spiritual achievements.
  • 世人無遠識見多驚異故吾所不為耳 - Means 'people have no far-sightedness; they are often surprised by unusual things, so I do not do such things,' explaining his actions.
  • 吾今死去徒众好住便還房内 - Means 'now that I am dead, the disciples should live well and return to their rooms,' indicating his final instructions.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 隋 - Refers to the Sui Dynasty, a period in Chinese history.
  • 蒋州 - Refers to Jiangzhou, a region in ancient China.
  • 大归善寺 - Refers to the Great Guishan Temple, a significant Buddhist temple.
  • 曲阿 - Refers to a place name, possibly a town or city.
  • 岭南 - Refers to the southern regions of China, known for its warm climate.
  • 栖霞 - Refers to Qixia, a place name, possibly a temple or region.
  • 扬都 - Refers to Yangzhou, an important city in eastern China.
  • 偲法师 - Refers to Master Si, a notable Buddhist monk.
  • 大业元年 - Refers to the first year of the Dali era, a specific period during the Sui Dynasty.

Proper Nouns

  • 釋慧偘 - The name of the Buddhist monk, transliterated as 'Hui Chan.'
  • 隋 - The Sui Dynasty.
  • 蒋州 - Jiangzhou.
  • 大归善寺 - The Great Guishan Temple.
  • 曲阿 - A place name.
  • 岭南 - The southern regions of China.
  • 栖霞 - Qixia.
  • 扬都 - Yangzhou.
  • 偲法师 - Master Si.
  • 大业元年 - The first year of the Dali era.

[1421]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 博通經敎 - Broadly learned in scriptures and teachings, indicating extensive knowledge of religious and philosophical texts.
  • 洞明防 - Profoundly understands the mysteries, referring to deep insight into esoteric or spiritual matters.
  • 耆艾請積繼之 - Elderly and respected members of the community invited Dào Jī to continue the project.
  • 廣行縁化 - Widely carry out fundraising, where '縁化' is a term for fundraising or collecting donations.
  • 槐檀十遷而大像成就 - '槐檀' likely refers to specific types of wood, and '十遷' means 'ten changes' or 'ten transformations,' suggesting a significant effort and transformation process.
  • 道俗慶賴 - Both monks and laypeople rejoiced and relied upon it, indicating widespread gratitude and support.
  • 感徹人天 - The impact reached both humans and deities, suggesting a profound and far-reaching effect.
  • 夢二狮子于大像侧连吐明珠相续不絶 - '二狮子' means 'two lions,' '大像侧' means 'beside the large statue,' '连吐明珠' means 'continuously spitting out pearls,' and '相续不絶' means 'continuously without interruption,' describing a vivid dream.
  • 宜运潜符徴效斯在 - '宜运' means 'appropriate timing,' '潜符' means 'hidden signs,' '徴效' means 'proofs and effects,' and '斯在' means 'are present,' suggesting that the dream was a sign of divine approval.
  • 即命工匠图所梦于弥勒大像前 - '即命工匠' means 'immediately ordered the craftsmen,' '图所梦' means 'to depict the dream,' and '弥勒大像前' means 'in front of the large statue of Maitreya,' indicating the creation of a visual representation of the dream.
  • 其寺在蒲坂之阳高爽华敞 - '蒲坂之阳' means 'on the sunny side of Puzhan,' '高爽' means 'elevated and refreshing,' and '华敞' means 'magnificent and spacious,' describing the location and appearance of the temple.
  • 东临州里南望河山 - '东临州里' means 'eastward overlooking the town,' and '南望河山' means 'southward looking at the rivers and mountains,' providing a geographical description.
  • 像设三层岩廊四合 - '像设三层' means 'statues arranged in three layers,' and '岩廊四合' means 'rocky corridors forming a quadrangle,' describing the architectural layout.
  • 上方下院赫奕相临 - '上方下院' means 'upper hall and lower courtyard,' and '赫奕相临' means 'glowing and adjacent,' indicating the grandeur and proximity of the structures.
  • 园硙田蔬周环俯就 - '园硙' means 'gardens and mills,' '田蔬' means 'fields and vegetables,' and '周环俯就' means 'surrounding and accessible,' describing the surrounding facilities.
  • 佛事隆盛咸积之功焉 - '佛事隆盛' means 'prosperous Buddhist activities,' and '咸积之功焉' means 'all due to Dào Jī's efforts,' attributing the success to Dào Jī's contributions.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 释道积 - Refers to Dào Jī, a monk known for his contributions to the construction of a large statue.
  • 唐蒲州普济寺 - Refers to the Puji Temple in Puzhou during the Tang Dynasty, a significant religious site.
  • 河东安邑县 - Refers to Anyi County in Hedong, a region in northern China.
  • 沙门宝澄 - Refers to Bǎo Chéng, another monk who initiated the construction of the large statue.
  • 大像 - Refers to a large statue, likely a Buddha statue, which was a significant religious and cultural symbol.
  • 弥勒大像 - Refers to a large statue of Maitreya, the future Buddha, indicating the religious significance of the project.
  • 法苑珠林 - A collection of Buddhist stories and teachings, providing the context for the narrative.

Proper Nouns

  • 释道积 - Dào Jī, the monk who continued the construction of the large statue.
  • 唐蒲州普济寺 - Puji Temple in Puzhou, a significant religious site.
  • 河东安邑县 - Anyi County in Hedong, a region in northern China.
  • 沙门宝澄 - Bǎo Chéng, the monk who initiated the construction of the large statue.
  • 大像 - The large statue, likely a Buddha statue.
  • 弥勒大像 - The large statue of Maitreya, the future Buddha.
  • 法苑珠林 - A collection of Buddhist stories and teachings.

[1423]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 太平广记 - A comprehensive collection of ancient Chinese stories and anecdotes, often used as a reference for historical and cultural narratives.
  • 钦定四库全书 - The Four Treasuries, a massive compilation of Chinese literature commissioned by the Qing dynasty, symbolizing the pinnacle of Chinese scholarship.
  • 报应十四 - A section of the *Tai Ping Guang Ji* focusing on stories of karma and retribution.
  • 久不欲外人知 - A phrase indicating that the monk has been in seclusion for a long time and does not wish to be known by outsiders.
  • 志心懴悔 - A sincere expression of repentance, often used in religious or moral contexts.
  • 以薪木瘗之 - A literal description of burying someone with firewood, implying a humble burial.
  • 初有两人来取 - The beginning of a supernatural encounter, where two figures appear to take the person.
  • 官曹门闾 - Administrative offices and gates, suggesting a bureaucratic or celestial setting.
  • 不可胜数 - An expression indicating an overwhelming number, emphasizing the grandeur or complexity of the scene.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 贞观十一年 - The eleventh year of the reign of Emperor Taizong of Tang (637 AD), a period known for its cultural and political achievements.
  • 华山 - One of the Five Great Mountains of China, often associated with immortals and spiritual practices.
  • 僧 - A Buddhist monk, often depicted as a wise and enlightened figure.
  • 净浴 - A ritual purification, often performed before religious or spiritual practices.
  • 被僧衣 - Wearing monastic robes, indicating a commitment to a spiritual path.
  • 懴悔 - Repentance, a key concept in Buddhism and other religious traditions.
  • 华州郑县 - A specific location in ancient China, providing a geographical context for the story.
  • 贫无棺椁 - A description of a poor burial, emphasizing the humility of the character.
  • 官府 - Government offices, suggesting a bureaucratic or celestial setting.
  • 巡巷 - Walking through alleys, a common motif in Chinese literature to describe journeys or explorations.
  • 曹院 - An administrative office, suggesting a bureaucratic or celestial setting.
  • 使者 - A messenger or envoy, often depicted as a figure of authority or importance.

  • 取法义前案簿盈一牀 - Means 'to retrieve Fa Yi's previous case records, which filled a table.'

  • 其簿多先朱勾毕 - Means 'most of the records were already marked with red checks.'

  • 合杖八十 - Means 'should receive eighty lashes' as a punishment.

  • 经懴悔者此案勾了 - Means 'those who have repented, their cases are already marked as complete.'
  • 至如张目骂父 - Means 'as for opening one's eyes and cursing one's father.'
  • 虽蒙懴悔事未勾了 - Means 'even though he has repented, the matter is not yet marked as complete.'
  • 若不如此当取案勘之 - Means 'if not so, then the case should be examined.'
  • 应有福利仰判官令典将法义过王宫殿宇宏壮 - Means 'there should be blessings; the judge instructed the clerk to bring Fa Yi to the magnificent palace.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 此人宿罪并贫道勾讫未合死 - This person's past sins have been settled by me, and he does not deserve to die.
  • 主典又以张目视父事过王 - The official then looked at the father with wide eyes and passed the matter to the king.
  • 张目懴悔此不合免 - Wide-eyed repentance, this does not warrant exemption.
  • 然师为来请可放七日 - However, since the master has come to request, he can be released for seven days.
  • 法义白僧曰日既不多后来恐不见师请即往随师 - Fayi told the monk, 'Since there is not much time left, I am afraid I will not see the master again. Please go with me.'
  • 七日七年也可早去 - Seven days or seven years, it is best to leave early.
  • 法义固请随僧因请王笔书法义掌中作一字 - Fayi insisted on going with the monk and asked the king for a pen to write a character on Fayi's palm.
  • 又请王印印之曰可急去还家凭作福报后来不见我宜以掌印呈王王自放汝也 - and asked the king to stamp it, saying, 'Go quickly back home. Rely on good deeds for your future. If you do not see me again, show this palm with the stamp to the king, and he will release you.'
  • 法义乃辞出僧令送出至其家内至黑义不敢入使者去之遂活觉在土中甚轻薄以手推排得出因入山就僧修福义掌中所印处文不可识然皆为疮终莫能愈至今尚存焉 - Fayi then left. The monk sent him out to his home. Inside, it was dark, and Fayi did not dare to enter. The messenger left, and Fayi woke up, feeling very light and thin. He pushed and pulled himself out of the ground and went into the mountains to seek the monk for blessings. The character on Fayi's palm was not recognizable, but it turned into sores, which never healed and still exist today.

  • 於和川病卒 - Means 'died of illness in Hechuan.'
  • 經数十日 - Means 'after several tens of days.'
  • 其家忽于夜中闻崔语 - Means 'his family suddenly heard Cui speaking in the middle of the night.'
  • 初时倾家惊恐 - Means 'at first, the whole family was terrified.'
  • 其后乃以为常 - Means 'later, they came to accept it as normal.'
  • 轨是女壻 - Means 'Gui is the son-in-law.'
  • 虽不合于妻家立灵 - Means 'though it is not proper to establish a shrine for the deceased in the wife's family.'
  • 苦无所依 - Means 'if there is no place to rely on.'
  • 但为置立也 - Means 'just set it up.'
  • 朝夕置食 - Means 'place food for him every day and night.'

  • 轨虽无罪然大资福助 - Means 'Gui, though he has no sin, greatly helps in accumulating merit.'
  • 为轨数设斋供 - Means 'set up several vegetarian meals for Gui.'
  • 并写法华金刚观音等经各三两部 - Means 'and write three or two copies of the Lotus Sutra, the Diamond Sutra, and the Guanyin Sutra.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 神龙年中 - Refers to the year 705 AD during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 襄阳将铸佛像 - Refers to the city of Xiangyang in Hubei Province, known for its historical significance, and the practice of casting Buddha statues.
  • 有一老姥至贫营求助施卒不成 - Refers to an elderly woman who was extremely poor and sought help and donations but failed to achieve her goal.
  • 得姥有一钱则为女时母所赐也 - The old woman had one coin which was given to her by her mother when she was a girl.
  • 宝之六十余年 - She treasured it for over sixty years.
  • 投之炉中 - She threw it into the furnace as a symbolic act of offering.
  • 因发重愿 - She made a vow, indicating her deep faith and commitment.
  • 破炉出像 - Breaking open the furnace to reveal the statue, a miraculous event.
  • 着佛胸臆 - On the chest of the Buddha, indicating the precise location of the coin.
  • 磨错去之 - Grinding away and removing it, a ritual act.
  • 一夕钱又如故 - The coin returned to its original state, a miraculous event.
  • 僧徒惊异 - Monks and disciples were astonished, indicating the impact of the event.
  • 钱至今存焉 - The coin is still preserved today, emphasizing its enduring significance.
  • 至诚发心 - Sincere heart, indicating the woman's genuine faith.
  • 诚应 - True response, indicating the fulfillment of her vow.
  • 心至诚故诸佛感之 - Because of her sincere heart, the Buddhas were moved by it.
  • 令后人生希有此事也 - Let future generations rarely experience this, emphasizing the rarity and significance of the event.
  • 普贤社 - A community dedicated to the bodhisattva Avalokiteshvara (Guanyin).
  • 开元初 - The early years of Emperor Xuanzong's reign, a period of cultural and economic prosperity.
  • 同州界 - A region in Shaanxi Province, known for its historical significance.
  • 数百家 - Hundreds of households, indicating the size of the community.
  • 为东西普贤邑社 - To be east and west communities dedicated to the bodhisattva Avalokiteshvara (Guanyin).
  • 造普贤菩萨像 - To create a statue of the bodhisattva Avalokiteshvara (Guanyin).
  • 每日设斋 - Every day, setting up a vegetarian meal.
  • 青衣以斋日生子 - A servant girl gave birth to a son on the day of the vegetarian meal.
  • 名之曰普贤 - To name him after the bodhisattva Avalokiteshvara (Guanyin).
  • 年至十八任为愚竖厮役之事 - At the age of eighteen, he was assigned to perform menial tasks.
  • 备尝 - To have experienced, indicating the hardships he faced.
  • 因设斋之日 - Because of the day of the vegetarian meal.
  • 此竖忽推普贤身像而坐其处 - This man suddenly pushed the statue of the bodhisattva Avalokiteshvara (Guanyin) and sat in its place.
  • 邑老观者咸用怒焉 - The village elders and spectators were all angry.
  • 既加诟骂又苦鞭挞 - Then added scolding and severe whipping.
  • 普贤笑曰 - The bodhisattva Avalokiteshvara (Guanyin) laughed and said.
  • 吾以汝志心故生此中 - I was born here because of your sincere heart.
  • 汝见真普贤不能加敬而求此土像何益 - You see the true bodhisattva but cannot show respect, and seeking this earthly image is of no benefit.
  • 于是忽变其质为普贤菩萨身 - Then suddenly changed his form into the form of the bodhisattva Avalokiteshvara (Guanyin).
  • 放大光明天花防云五 - Emitting great light, heavenly flowers, clouds, and five colors.

[1429]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 色相映于是遂灭 - The appearance of something caused something to disappear.
  • 邑老方悟贤圣大用惊惭 - The elders finally realized the great use of the holy ones, feeling amazed and ashamed.
  • 其西社为普贤邑斋者僧徒方集 - The Western community of the town, known as the Pure Land of Universal Worthy, where monks and disciples were gathering.
  • 忽有妇人怀姙垂产云见欲生子因入菩萨堂中 - Suddenly, a pregnant woman who was about to give birth appeared and said she wanted to give birth to a son, so she entered the hall of the Bodhisattva.
  • 人呵怒之不可禁止 - People scolded and angrily tried to stop her, but they couldn't.
  • 因产一男子于座之前既初产生甚为污秽诸人不可提挈出深用诟辱 - She gave birth to a boy in front of the seat, and at first, the birth was very messy, causing people to deeply insult and humiliate him.
  • 忽失妇人所在男变为普贤菩萨光明照烛相好端丽其所污秽皆成香花 - Suddenly, the woman disappeared, and the boy transformed into the Bodhisattva Universal Worthy, whose light illuminated everything, and his appearance was beautiful and dignified. All the mess turned into fragrant flowers.
  • 于是乗象腾空稍稍而灭 - Then, he rode on an elephant into the sky and gradually disappeared.
  • 诸父老自恨愚闇不识普贤刺眇其目者十余人由是言之菩萨变现岂凡人能识 - The elders regretted their ignorance and lack of recognition of the Bodhisattva, and more than ten people who had blinded themselves with needles said that the transformation of the Bodhisattva could not be recognized by ordinary people.

  • 普贤菩萨 - A bodhisattva in Mahayana Buddhism, known for his vow to achieve enlightenment for the benefit of all sentient beings.
  • 阎罗王 - Yama, the King of Hell, a figure in Buddhist and Hindu mythology who judges the dead.
  • 金光明经 - The *Golden Light Sutra*, a significant Mahayana Buddhist scripture that emphasizes the importance of the Buddha's teachings and the benefits of reciting and studying the sutra.
  • 山人 - A term for a hermit or recluse, often associated with scholars or poets who live in seclusion.

[1430]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 为何城吏云安禄山作乱所司恐贼越逸故作此城以遏之 - Complex sentence describing the reason for the city's construction: '为何' means 'why', '城吏云' means 'the city officials say', '安禄山作乱' refers to An Lushan's rebellion, '所司恐贼越逸' means 'the authorities fear the rebels will escape', and '故作此城以遏之' means 'so they built this city to block them'.
  • 又问城主为谁曰是邬元昌洽素与城主有故请为通之 - Describes a dialogue and a request: '又问' means 'and asked', '城主为谁' means 'who the city's master is', '曰是邬元昌' means 'he said it is Wu Yuanzhang', '洽素与城主有故' means 'who has a long-standing relationship with the city's master', and '请为通之' means 'and asked to be introduced'.
  • 元昌召入相见悲喜须臾有兵马数十万至城而过 - Describes a sequence of events: '元昌召入相见' means 'Wu Yuanzhang summoned him in to meet', '悲喜' means 'joy and sorrow', '须臾' means 'a short time later', and '有兵马数十万至城而过' means 'there were hundreds of thousands of troops passing through the city'.
  • 元昌留洽坐出门迎候久之乃回 - Describes a series of actions: '元昌留洽坐' means 'Wu Yuanzhang kept him seated', '出门迎候' means 'went out to greet', '久之乃回' means 'after a long time, he returned'.
  • 此兵云何曰阎罗王往西京大安国寺也 - Introduces a question and its answer: '此兵云何' means 'what do these soldiers say', '曰阎罗王' means 'they said it is Yama', '往西京大安国寺' means 'going to the Great An Guo Temple in the Western Capital'.
  • 既至寺登百尺高座王将簿阅云此人新造金光明经遂得延算故未合死 - Describes a sequence of events: '既至寺' means 'having arrived at the temple', '登百尺高座' means 'he ascended a hundred-foot high seat', '王将簿阅' means 'the king reviewed the records', '云此人新造金光明经' means 'saying this person newly created the *Golden Light Sutra*', '遂得延算' means 'thus extending his life', '故未合死' means 'so he should not die'.
  • 令人送囘因此得活 - Describes a conclusion to the previous narrative: '令人送囘' means 'he sent someone to escort him back', '因此得活' means 'thus he was saved'.
  • 王乙者自少恒持如意轮咒开元初徒侣三人将适北河有船夫求载乙等不甚论钱直云正尔自行故不计价 - Introduces Wang Yi and his companions: '王乙者自少恒持如意轮咒' means 'Wang Yi has always held the *Sukhavati-vyuha* mantra since childhood', '开元初' means 'in the early Kaiyuan period', '徒侣三人' means 'three companions', '将适北河' means 'were heading to the North River', '有船夫求载乙等' means 'a boatman asked to carry them', '不甚论钱直' means 'not very concerned about money', '云正尔自行' means 'saying they would just go themselves', '故不计价' means 'so they didn't count the cost'.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 常修德不耀人，莫知之家 - This phrase can be read as 'always cultivates virtue without showing it off, and no one knows about his family,' indicating a private and humble life.
  • 施人之惠不望报 - This phrase can be read as 'giving help to others without expecting repayment,' reflecting a generous and selfless attitude.
  • 长庆初有善相人于寿春道逢元溥 - This sentence is elliptical and can be read as 'In the early years of the Changqing era, a good fortune-teller met Yuan Pu on the road to Shouchun.'
  • 君财甚丰矣，然更二三年大期将至 - This sentence is a concessive pattern, acknowledging the person's wealth but warning of an impending significant event.
  • 涕泗曰：夫寿夭者天也，先生其奈我何 - This sentence is a direct speech, with the speaker expressing resignation and fatalism.
  • 相不及德德不及度量 - This phrase can be read as 'Appearance does not match virtue, and virtue does not match one's capacity,' emphasizing the importance of inner qualities over outward appearance.
  • 告后事，但二三年之期勤修令德，冀或延之 - This sentence is a conditional statement, advising the person to cultivate virtue to perhaps extend their life.
  • 一德可以消百灾，犹享爵禄而况于寿乎 - This sentence is a comparative statement, emphasizing the value of virtue in achieving a long life.
  • 勉而图之，吾三载当复此来 - This sentence is a motivational statement followed by a promise or prediction.
  • 言讫而去 - This phrase is a concise description of the fortune-teller's departure.
  • 流涕送 - This phrase is a vivid description of the emotional reaction of Duan Hongjing.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 躬率百夫直冒矢石 - Classical structure; '躬率' means 'personally led,' '百夫' is 'a hundred men' (military unit), '直冒矢石' means 'braved arrows and stones.' Describes bravery in battle.
  • 处女三人蔚有姿容 - '蔚有姿容' is a classical way of saying 'had graceful and beautiful appearances'; '蔚' is an archaic adverb meaning 'indeed' or 'clearly.'
  • 浃旬间 - Fixed time expression meaning 'within ten days.'
  • 父母持金请赎 - '持金' is 'brought gold' or 'carried money,' and '请赎' is 'asked to redeem' or 'requested to buy back their daughters.' Formal expression of a request for redemption.
  • 公还金归女 - '公' refers to the official (程彦賔), '还金' is 'returned the gold,' '归女' is 'returned the daughters.' Concise but clear sentence.
  • 告以全人 - '告' is 'told,' '以' is 'to,' and '全人' is 'a complete person' or 'an unblemished person.' In context, it means 'told them that the daughters were unblemished.'
  • 愿公早建旄节 - '旄节' is a term for a military standard or symbol of authority. '建旄节' is 'to establish a military command' or 'to hold a high position.' '愿公早建' is 'May you soon hold such a position.'
  • 吾所愿寿终时无病耳 - '吾所愿' is 'what I desire,' '寿终时' is 'at the time of my death,' '无病耳' is 'without illness, that's all.' Humble and self-deprecating expression.
  • 后年逾耳顺果无疾而终 - '年逾耳顺' refers to the age of 60 (traditional Chinese term for 60, as the ear is said to be 'in harmony' at that age). '无疾而终' is a common phrase meaning 'died without illness.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 伪蜀 - Refers to the state of Shu, a short-lived kingdom established by Wang Jian in 907 after the fall of the Tang Dynasty. Called '伪' (false or pseudo) because it was not recognized by the legitimate Song Dynasty.
  • 罗城 - A type of fortified city wall. In this context, it refers to a city under the control of the Shu regime.
  • 处女三人 - Refers to three unmarried women. In this context, they are likely captives taken during the siege.
  • 儆戒録 - A classical Chinese collection of moral tales or cautionary stories. Similar to *The Book of Rewards and Punishments* or *Jingjie Lu* (儆戒录), which contains stories meant to illustrate moral lessons.
  • 太平广记 - A massive compendium of Chinese tales, stories, and anecdotes from various historical sources, compiled during the Song Dynasty. One of the most important sources of classical Chinese folklore and supernatural stories.
  • 报应十七 - Section title in *太平广记*, meaning 'Recompense 17,' which refers to the 17th chapter or section on karma or retribution.

[1475]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 不作劫陶令已当具知枉见杀害 - Complex phrase; '不作劫' implies not acting as a bandit or committing a robbery, but in context, it suggests the person was not a criminal. '陶令' refers to Tao Qian, a famous poet and official. '已当具知枉见杀害' means he should have known he was being wrongfully killed.
  • 若无则已有必自陈诉 - Conditional phrase; '若无' can be interpreted as 'if there are no ghosts,' but in context, it might mean 'if there is no ghost.' '必自陈诉' means 'will surely come forward to complain.'
  • 因弹琵琶歌数曲而就死 - Sequence of events; '因弹琵琶歌数曲' means 'because he played the pipa and sang several songs.' '而就死' means 'and then died.'
  • 众知其枉莫不陨泣 - Emotional impact; '众知其枉' means 'the people knew he was wronged.' '莫不陨泣' means 'no one did not shed tears.'
  • 经月余陶遂梦伎来至案前 - Dream sequence; '经月余' means 'after more than a month.' '陶遂梦伎来至案前' means 'Tao Qian then dreamed that a performer came to his desk.'
  • 云昔枉见杀实所不忿诉天得理 - Dialogue within the dream; '云昔枉见杀' means 'said that he was wrongfully killed.' '实所不忿' means 'which he truly resented.' '诉天得理' means 'complained to heaven and received justice.'
  • 今故取君便跳入陶口乃落腹中 - Action in the dream; '今故取君' means 'now he comes to take you.' '便跳入陶口' means 'jumped into Tao Qian's mouth.' '乃落腹中' means 'and fell into his stomach.'
  • 陶即惊寤俄而倒状若风癫 - Transition from dream to reality; '陶即惊寤' means 'Tao Qian immediately woke up in shock.' '俄而倒状若风癫' means 'soon collapsed and appeared to be possessed by a wind demon.'
  • 良久苏醒有时而发发即夭矫头乃着背 - Description of symptoms; '良久苏醒' means 'after a long time, he woke up.' '有时而发' means 'sometimes it would flare up.' '发即夭矫头乃着背' means 'when it flared up, his head would twist and stick to his back.'
  • 四日而亡亡后家便贫瘁二儿早死余有一孙穷寒路次 - Sequence of events after death; '四日而亡' means 'died four days later.' '亡后家便贫瘁' means 'after his death, the family became poor and destitute.' '二儿早死' means 'his two sons died early.' '余有一孙穷寒路次' means 'he left behind a grandson who was poor and lived on the streets.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 防稽孔基 - Refers to a person named Kong Ji from the region of Fangqi (modern-day Shaoxing, Zhejiang).
  • 勤學有志操 - Describes Kong Ji as diligent in study and steadfast in character.
  • 族人孔敞 - Refers to a relative named Kong Chang, who is a member of the same clan as Kong Ji.
  • 使其二子從基為師 - Means 'he made his two sons follow Kong Ji as their teacher,' indicating Kong Chang's action of sending his sons to learn from Kong Ji.
  • 凶狠趋向不轨 - Describes the sons' behavior as 'cruel, ruthless, and lawless,' suggesting they had a tendency toward wrongdoing.
  • 基屡言于敞 - Means 'Kong Ji repeatedly spoke to Kong Chang,' indicating Kong Ji's attempts to warn Kong Chang about his sons' behavior.
  • 此儿常有忿志 - Means 'this boy often harbors resentment,' referring to the sons' internal anger or dissatisfaction.
  • 敞寻丧亡 - Means 'Kong Chang soon passed away,' indicating his death.
  • 服制既除 - Refers to the end of the mourning period, when the usual rituals and restrictions after a death are completed.
  • 基以宿旧乃赍羊酒往看二子 - Means 'Kong Ji, out of old friendship, brought sheep and wine to visit the two sons,' showing his attempt to maintain a relationship with them despite their behavior.
  • 二子犹怀宿怨 - Means 'the two sons still harbored old grudges,' indicating their continued resentment toward Kong Ji.
  • 潜遣奴路侧杀基 - Means 'they secretly sent a servant to kill Kong Ji on the road,' describing the plot against him.
  • 奴还未至乃见基来 - Means 'the servant had not yet arrived when Kong Ji came,' indicating that the servant's mission failed because Kong Ji appeared unexpectedly.
  • 张目攘袂厉声言曰 - Means 'he opened his eyes, flared his sleeves, and spoke in a stern voice,' describing Kong Ji's dramatic and forceful reaction.
  • 奸丑小竖人面兽心 - Means 'you are a despicable little boy with the face of a human and the heart of a beast,' a harsh criticism of the sons' character.
  • 吾蒙顾存昔敦旧平生有何怨恶 - Means 'I was once your teacher and friend, and we had a close relationship. What grievances do you have against me?' Kong Ji is questioning the sons' reasons for plotting against him.
  • 候道见害反天忘父人神不容 - Means 'to ambush me on the road, betray the heavens, and forget your father—this is something even the gods cannot tolerate,' a strong condemnation of their actions.
  • 要当断汝家种 - Means 'I will make sure to cut off your lineage,' a threat to the sons' family line.
  • 从此之后数日见形 - Means 'after several days, Kong Ji appeared as a ghost,' suggesting that he was killed and returned as a vengeful spirit.
  • 孔氏无几大儿向厠忽便绝倒 - Means 'the eldest son of the Kong family suddenly collapsed while using the toilet,' indicating a sudden and fatal event.
  • 络绎往看已毙于地 - Means 'people came in a steady stream to see him, but he was already dead on the ground,' describing the aftermath of the eldest son's death.
  • 次者寻复病疽而死 - Means 'the younger son soon fell ill with an abscess and died,' indicating the younger son's subsequent death.
  • 卒致兄弟无后 - Means 'ultimately, the brothers left no descendants,' concluding the tragic outcome.
  • 昙摩懴 - Refers to a Buddhist monk named Tanmo Chan.
  • 沮渠蒙逊时 - Refers to the period when Juqu Mengxun was in power.
  • 沙门昙摩懴者 - Refers to the monk Tanmo Chan, who was a Buddhist monk.
  • 博达多识 - Means 'broadly knowledgeable and well-versed,' describing Tanmo Chan's qualities.
  • 为蒙逊之所信重 - Means 'he was trusted and highly regarded by Juqu Mengxun,' indicating the monk's status.
  • 魏氏遣李顺拜蒙逊为凉王 - Means 'the Wei dynasty sent Li Shun to confer the title of King of Liang on Juqu Mengxun,' describing a diplomatic event.
  • 乃求昙摩懴 - Means 'they then sought Tanmo Chan,' indicating the Wei dynasty's interest in the monk.
  • 昙摩懴意欲入魏 - Means 'Tanmo Chan wished to enter the Wei dynasty,' expressing his desire to go to the Wei court.
  • 屡从蒙逊请行 - Means 'he repeatedly asked Juqu Mengxun for permission to go,' showing his persistent requests.
  • 蒙逊怒杀之 - Means 'Juqu Mengxun became angry and killed him,' describing the tragic end of Tanmo Chan.
  • 既而左右当白日中见昙摩懴以剑击蒙逊因以疾而死 - Means 'soon afterward, his attendants saw Tanmo Chan in broad daylight strike Juqu Mengxun with a sword, and Juqu Mengxun died from his injuries,' describing the monk's vengeful appearance and Juqu Mengxun's death.

[1478]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 庾宏為竟陵王府佐家在江陵 - Indicates that Yu Hong served as a household official for the Prince of Jingling and lived in Jiangling. '府佐' (household official) is a formal title indicating a position within a noble household.
  • 宏令奴無患者 - Yu Hong ordered his servant, who was not ill, to carry rice to his family. '無患者' (not ill) is an adjective modifying the servant, indicating that the servant was healthy.
  • 遭劫被杀尸流泊查口村 - Describes a tragic event where the servant was robbed, killed, and his body floated to a village called Cha Kou Village. '遭劫被杀' (robbed and killed) and '尸流泊' (body floated) are vivid descriptions of the servant's fate.
  • 岸傍有文欣者母病医云须得髑髅屑服之即差 - There was a person named Wen Xin by the shore. '母病' (mother was ill) and '医云须得髑髅屑服之即差' (the doctor said that to cure her, she needed to take some bone powder from a skull) describe a medical condition and a prescribed remedy.
  • 欣重赏募索有邻妇杨氏见无患尸因断头与欣 - Wen Xin offered a reward to search for the body. '邻妇杨氏见无患尸因断头与欣' (a neighbor's wife named Yang saw the servant's body and cut off his head to give to Wen Xin) describes the neighbor's action.
  • 烧之欲去皮肉经三日夜不焦眼角张转 - Burned it to remove the flesh. '经三日夜不焦眼角张转' (after three days and nights, it did not burn, and the eyes opened and turned) describes the unusual behavior of the burned skull.
  • 杨氏以语儿言终而卒 - Yang told her son, and she eventually died.
  • 北齐阳翟太守张善苛酷贪叨恶声流布 - Zhang Shan, the governor of Yangzhai during the Northern Qi dynasty, was cruel, greedy, and notorious.
  • 兰台遣御史魏辉儁就郡治之 - The Office of History sent a censor named Wei Huijun to investigate the case.
  • 赃贿狼籍罪当合死 - Corruption and bribery; guilty of death.
  • 善于狱中使人通诉反诬辉儁为纳民财枉见推缚 - Zhang Shan, while in prison, sent someone to report and accused Wei Huijun of accepting bribes and unjustly imprisoning him.
  • 文宣帝大怒以为法司阿曲必须穷正 - Emperor Wenxuan was furious and believed the legal officials were biased and needed to be thoroughly investigated.
  • 令尚书令左丞卢斐覆验之 - Ordered the Left Censor of the Ministry of Works, Lu Fei, to re-examine the case.
  • 斐遂希防成辉儁罪状奏报于州斩决 - Lu Fei then fabricated charges against Wei Huijun and reported them to the state, leading to his execution.
  • 卢令史哀悼为之殡敛并备纸笔十五日善得病唯云叩头未旬日而死才两月卢斐坐讥駮魏史为魏奏文宣帝鸩杀之 - Lu, the clerk, mourned and arranged for his burial. Prepared paper and pens for fifteen days; Zhang Shan fell ill and only said 'kneel' before dying within ten days. After two months, Lu Fei was accused of criticizing Wei Huijun's history and was poisoned by Emperor Wenxuan.

  • 侯景生焉 - Literal meaning: 'Hou Jing was born.' Refers to the birth of Hou Jing, who would later become a significant figure in the Liang dynasty.
  • 簡文幽而壓死 - Literal meaning: 'Jianwen was imprisoned and died under pressure.' Describes the fate of Emperor Jianwen, who was imprisoned and died under duress.
  • 鄰人忿之乃焚其屋 - Literal meaning: 'The neighbor, angry, burned down his house.' Describes the neighbor's violent reaction to Zhang Bi's refusal of his proposal.
  • 潛同凶黨 - Literal meaning: 'secretly aligned with the wicked party.' Refers to someone secretly collaborating with a group of evil people.
  • 鄰人又見禆排門戶直入 - Literal meaning: 'The neighbor saw Zhang Bi pushing open the doors and entering directly.' Describes Zhang Bi's ghostly appearance to the neighbor.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 梁武帝蕭衍 - Refers to Emperor Wu of Liang (464–549), a ruler of the Liang dynasty in China.
  • 東昏侯 - Refers to Emperor Donghuang Hou (483–501), the last ruler of the Southern Qi dynasty.
  • 侯景 - Refers to Hou Jing (503–552), a general who later rebelled against the Liang dynasty.
  • 簡文 - Refers to Emperor Jianwen (503–551), the son of Emperor Wu of Liang.
  • 隋庶人勇 - Refers to Yang Yong (572–604), the crown prince of the Sui dynasty, who was demoted to a commoner.
  • 京兆獄卒卭人 - Refers to a prison guard from the region of Jingzhao (modern-day Xi'an) during the Sui dynasty.

One night, the jailer dreamed of a tall man with a dark face accompanied by over twenty others who took Governor Chu away. Idiomatic Expressions

  • 合赦殊死遂释犹恨而斩绍先 - Combined amnesty and pardon for capital crimes, despite release, there was still resentment, and Zhao Xian was executed.
  • 吾善李邈须死而言 - I am good friends with Li Miao, who is about to die and wants to speak.
  • 吾自幼即具知前生事 - I have known about my past life since I was young.
  • 明日讲武吾其不免吾前世为某氏女既笄适灞陵王氏子为妻姑待吾甚严 - The next day, during the military drill, I will not escape death; in my past life, I was a woman from a certain family, after reaching adulthood, I married a son of the Wang family from Ba Ling, and my mother-in-law treated me very strictly.
  • 吾年十七冬至先一日姑令吾躬具主馔比毕吾困怠亦甚姑又令吾缝罗裙迟明服以待客吾临灯运针虑功之不就夜分不息忽一犬冲扉入房触灯灯僵油仆裙上吾且惧且恨因叱犬犬走突扉而扉反阖犬周章却伏牀下吾复照烛将理裙污而狼藉殆遍吾惧姑深责且恨犬之触灯遂举 - When I was seventeen, the day before Winter Solstice, my mother-in-law ordered me to prepare the main meal, by the time I finished, I was very tired, then ordered me to sew a silk skirt to wear the next day for guests, as I worked by the light, I was worried that I wouldn't finish, and I didn't stop until midnight, suddenly, a dog rushed in through the door, knocked over the lamp, and spilled oil on my skirt, I was both afraid and angry, so I shouted at the dog, which ran back out, slamming the door shut, the dog circled around and lay down under the bed, and I lit the candle again to fix the mess on my skirt, which was almost completely ruined, I feared that my mother-in-law would severely criticize me and blamed the dog for knocking over the lamp, so I took a pair of scissors and stabbed the dog, accidentally hitting its neck, and the scissors broke; I then used the other half to stab it again, and the dog died.
  • 吾年十九而卒遂生于此身往者毙犬乃今之李邈也吾明日之死葢縁报也行戮者必是李邈乎报应葢理之常尔无骇焉 - I died at nineteen and was reborn in this life; the dog I killed in my past life was Li Miao in this life, my death tomorrow is probably a form of retribution, will the executioner be Li Miao? Retribution is a common principle, so don't be surprised.

  • 唐书 - The *Tang Shu*, an official history of the Tang Dynasty, which records historical events and figures.

  • 窃以李某善饮酒故请召之 - The prefect admits that he invited Li because of his skill in drinking, a somewhat informal reason.

  • 今明公既已诛之宜矣 - The prefect acknowledges that the deputy ambassador's execution of Li was appropriate.

  • 君无复言及宴罢太守密讯其年则二十有七矣 - The prefect is told not to speak further, and after the banquet, he discreetly inquires about Li's age, which turns out to be twenty-seven.
  • 葢李生杀少年之嵗而士真生于王氏也 - The prefect realizes that Li was the same age as the youth he had killed, and the deputy ambassador was born into the Wang family.
  • 太守叹异久之因以家财厚葬李生 - The prefect is astonished for a long time and eventually uses his own wealth to give Li a proper burial.

  • 及去母问之 - Means 'when they leave, the mother asks them,' indicating a follow-up question.
  • 答曰 - Means 'they answer,' introducing a response.
  • 某前生曾贩羊从夏州来至此翁庄宿父子三人并为其害劫其资货 - Complex sentence meaning 'In my previous life, I once traded sheep and came from Xiazhou to this old man's village, where the father and son were all killed and their goods were stolen,' describing a past life event.
  • 某前生乃与之作儿聦黠胜人渠甚爱念十五患病二十方卒 - Complex sentence meaning 'In my previous life, I was his son, cleverer than others, and he loved me very much. I fell ill at fifteen and only died at twenty,' describing the relationship and fate.
  • 前后用医药已过所劫数倍 - Means 'before and after, the medicine used exceeded the value of the stolen goods by several times,' indicating the extent of the medical expenses.
  • 渠又为某每歳亡日作斋夫妻涕泣计其泪过三两石矣 - Complex sentence meaning 'He also made offerings for me every year on the day of my death, and the couple wept, and the amount of tears they shed exceeded three or four stones,' describing the ongoing memorial activities.
  • 偶因僧问乞饭处某遂指导之耳 - Means 'By chance, when a monk asked where to beg for food, I pointed it out,' indicating a casual encounter.
  • 亦是偿债了矣 - Means 'it is also to repay the debt,' concluding the story with a moral reflection.
  • 翁姥从此更不复作斋也 - Means 'the old man and old woman no longer made offerings,' indicating the end of the memorial activities.

[1561]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 僧曰 - Common phrase used to introduce a monk's speech or statement.

  • 仍合家素飡 - Means 'and have the whole family eat vegetarian meals,' where '素飡' (su can) refers to a vegetarian diet.
  • 堂前设道请名僧昼夜诵经礼懴 - Means 'Set up a dais in front of the hall and invite famous monks to recite sutras and perform repentance rituals day and night,' where '道' (dao lai) refers to a dais or platform.
  • 可延百日之命 - Means 'which can extend your life by a hundred days,' where '延' (yan) means to extend.

  • 惟思曰诸事易耳然苦不食之何 - Means 'Wei Si said, 'All these things are easy, but how can I endure not eating meat?'' where '诸事' (zhu shi) means all these things, and '不食' (bu shi rou) means not eating meat.
  • 取羊肝水浸加以椒醤食之即能飡矣 - Means 'Take sheep liver, soak it in water, add pepper sauce, and eat it; then you can eat,' where '水浸' (shui jin) means to soak in water, and '椒醤' (jiao jiang) means pepper sauce.
  • 既觉具告其妻 - Means 'After waking up, he told his wife about it,' where '具告' (ju gao) means to tell in detail.
  • 妻赞之 - Means 'His wife praised him,' where '赞' (zan) means to praise.
  • 又如言置道场转经且食羊肝 - Means 'Then, as instructed, they set up the dharma assembly and recited sutras while also eating the sheep liver,' where '置道场' (zhi dao chang) means to set up a dharma assembly.
  • 如是月余 - Means 'Like this for over a month,' where '如是' (ru shi) means like this, and '月余' (yue yu) means over a month.
  • 晨坐其亡母亡姊皆来视之 - Means 'One morning, her deceased mother and sister came to see her,' where '晨坐' (chen zuo) means sitting in the morning, and '亡母亡姊' (wang mu wang ji) means deceased mother and sister.
  • 惟思大惊趋走迎候 - Means 'Wei Si was greatly surprised and hurried to greet them,' where '趋走' (qu zou) means to hurry.
  • 有一子手执綘旛前引升自西阶植綘旛焉 - Means 'There was a ghost child holding a red flag in front, leading them up the western steps and planting the red flag,' where '子' (gui zi) means ghost child, '綘旛' (jing fan) means red flag, and '升自西阶' (sheng zi xi jie) means to ascend the western steps.
  • 其亡姊不言但于旛下舞僛僛不辍 - Means 'Her deceased sister did not speak but danced continuously under the flag,' where '僛僛' (qi qi) means to dance, and '不辍' (bu chuo) means continuously.
  • 其母泣曰惟思在生不知罪杀人无数今寃家欲来吾不见汝受苦辛故来视汝 - Means 'Her mother cried and said, 'Wei Si, in life, you did not know the crime of killing countless people. Now, the victims' spirits want to come. I do not want to see you suffer, so I came to see you,' where '不知罪杀人无数' (bu zhi zui sha ren wu shu) means did not know the crime of killing countless people, and '寃家欲来' (yuan jia yu lai) means the victims' spirits want to come.
  • 惟思命设祭母母食之 - Means 'Wei Si ordered a sacrifice to be set up for her mother, and the mother ate it,' where '命设祭' (ming she ji) means to order a sacrifice to be set up.
  • 其姊舞更不已 - Means 'Her sister continued to dance,' where '不已' (bu yi) means continuously.
  • 不交一言 - Means 'They did not exchange a word,' where '不交一言' (bu jiao yi yan) means did not exchange a word.
  • 母食毕与姊皆去 - Means 'After the mother finished eating, both sisters left,' where '食毕' (shi bi) means after finishing eating.
  • 惟思疾转甚于是羊肝亦不食常卧道场 - Means 'Wei Si's illness worsened, and he no longer ate the sheep liver, lying down in the dharma assembly,' where '疾转甚' (ji zhuan shen) means the illness worsened, and '常卧道场' (chang wo dao chang) means lying down in the dharma assembly.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 家中钱物衣服尽用施寺 - Refers to the act of donating all household possessions to a temple, a common practice in traditional Chinese Buddhism.
  • 素飡 - Refers to a vegetarian diet, often practiced by Buddhists.
  • 道请名僧昼夜诵经礼懴 - Refers to the practice of setting up a dais in a temple and inviting renowned monks to recite sutras and perform repentance rituals, which are important in Buddhist rituals.
  • 延百日之命 - Refers to the belief that certain rituals can extend one's life, a common belief in traditional Chinese culture.
  • 羊肝水浸加以椒醤食之 - Refers to a specific dish made from sheep liver, which is described as a remedy for not being able to eat meat.
  • 晨坐其亡母亡姊皆来视之 - Refers to the belief in the afterlife and the appearance of deceased family members, a common theme in traditional Chinese beliefs.
  • 子手执綘旛前引升自西阶 - Refers to the appearance of a ghost child leading the deceased, a common motif in traditional Chinese ghost stories.
  • 亡母亡姊 - Refers to the deceased mother and sister, emphasizing the theme of family and the afterlife.
  • 寃家欲来 - Refers to the victims' spirits, a concept in traditional Chinese beliefs about justice and retribution in the afterlife.
  • 命设祭母母食之 - Refers to the practice of setting up a sacrifice and offering it to the deceased, a common ritual in traditional Chinese culture.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The long sentence '僧曰汝能度两子爲僧家中钱物衣服尽用施寺仍合家素飡堂前设道请名僧昼夜诵经礼懴可延百日之命如不能即当死矣' must be read as a single statement from the monk, outlining a series of actions and their consequences.
  • The clause '惟思曰诸事易耳然苦不食之何' is a response from Wei Si, expressing his difficulty in adhering to the dietary restrictions.
  • The clause '取羊肝水浸加以椒醤食之即能飡矣' provides a solution to Wei Si's problem, but it is presented as a temporary measure.
  • The clause '既觉具告其妻妻赞之' describes the communication between Wei Si and his wife, showing her support.
  • The clause '又如言置道场转经且食羊肝' describes the implementation of the monk's instructions, including both the religious and dietary aspects.
  • The clause '如是月余晨坐其亡母亡姊皆来视之' introduces the supernatural element, where the deceased family members appear.
  • The clause '惟思大惊趋走迎候有一子手执綘旛前引升自西阶植綘旛焉' describes the appearance of the ghost child and the planting of the red flag, a significant event in the narrative.
  • The clause '其亡姊不言但于旛下舞僛僛不辍' describes the continuous dance of Wei Si's deceased sister under the flag.
  • The clause '其母泣曰惟思在生不知罪杀人无数今寃家欲来吾不见汝受苦辛故来视汝' describes the mother's emotional reaction and her reasons for appearing to Wei Si.
  • The clause '惟思命设祭母母食之' describes the act of setting up a sacrifice for Wei Si's mother and her eating it.
  • The clause '其姊舞更不已' describes the continued dance of Wei Si's sister.
  • The clause '不交一言' indicates that there was no verbal communication between the deceased and Wei Si.
  • The clause '母食毕与姊皆去' describes the departure of Wei Si's mother and sister after the sacrifice.
  • The clause '惟思疾转甚于是羊肝亦不食常卧道场' describes the worsening of Wei Si's condition and his decision to stop eating the sheep liver and lie down in the dharma assembly.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 金巵 - A gold cup or chalice, often used metaphorically or as a valuable object.
  • 因妒忌打杀婢金巵 - Due to jealousy, [she] beat and killed the servant girl named Jin Chi. This phrase combines jealousy with a violent act, requiring careful rendering to capture the emotional and physical violence.
  • 潜于本家埋瘗 - Secretly buried at her own home. The verb 潜 (qian) implies secrecy or stealth, while 埋瘗 (mai yi) means 'to bury' or 'to inter.'
  • 牓通衢云婢金巵逃走 - Posted a notice in a public place saying that the servant girl Jin Chi had fled. 牓 (bang) means 'to post a notice,' 通衢 (tong qu) refers to a main thoroughfare or public square, and 云 (yun) is an archaic particle meaning 'say.'
  • 经年迁居夹江 - After a year, she moved to Jiajiang. 经年 (jing nian) means 'after a year' or 'for a long time.'
  • 因夏潦飘壊旧居渠岸 - Because of the summer floods, the old house and the riverbank were destroyed. 夏潦 (xia lao) refers to summer floods, and 飘壊 (piao huai) means 'to be destroyed by water.'
  • 见死婢容质不变 - She saw the dead servant girl's appearance and body unchanged. 容质 (rong zhi) refers to both appearance and physical form.
  • 鎭将具状报州追勘 - The magistrate prepared a report and reported it to the state, which then investigated. 具状 (ju zhuang) means 'to prepare a report,' 报州 (bao zhou) means 'to report to the state,' and 追勘 (zhui kan) means 'to investigate.'
  • 欵伏其婢尸一夕壊烂 - The servant girl's body rotted within one night. 款伏 (kuan fu) means 'to confess,' and 一夕 (yi xie) means 'one night.'
  • 遂寘丁氏于法 - Thus, Ding Shi was placed under the law. 寘 (zhi) means 'to place' or 'to punish,' and 于法 (yu fa) means 'under the law.'

  • 蜀青石鎭陈洪妻丁氏 - Refers to a woman named Ding Shi, the wife of Chen Hong, from Qingshi Town in Shu (Sichuan). The name 陈洪 (Chen Hong) is likely a historical figure, but without further context, it is difficult to provide additional information.
  • 金巵 - As mentioned, this refers to a gold cup, which could symbolize wealth or status.
  • 太平广记卷一百三十 - This indicates that the story is part of the *Taiping Guangji* (太平广记), a large collection of anecdotes and stories compiled during the Song Dynasty.
  • 钦定四库全书 - This refers to the *Qinding Siku Quanshu* (钦定四库全书), a massive compilation of Chinese literature commissioned by the Qing Dynasty.
